summary: Exhaustive audit: Figma (tokens/visual/UX) + web (tokens/a11y/responsive/perf), adversarially verified

figma-tokens.title: Semantic variable action/secondary has a raw (non-alias) value in Hi-Fi mode
figma-tokens.detail: The rule is that every Wireframe-collection semantic variable aliases a primitive in BOTH modes (raw scrim colors exempt). action/secondary aliases Primitives/grey/0 in Wireframe mode but its Hi-Fi mode value is a raw #FFFFFF @0% (fully transparent white), not an alias. It is not a scrim, so it is not covered by the known-intentional exemption. Fix option: add a whiteA/00 primitive and alias it, keeping the whiteA ramp the single source for white-alpha values.
figma-tokens.evidence: Dump: {Wireframe: alias:Primitives/grey/0, Hi-Fi: #FFFFFF@0%}. All 33 other semantic vars alias primitives in both modes except the 3 exempt surface/scrim* vars and border/danger (separate finding). 0 dangling aliases, 0 missing mode values.
figma-tokens.verifyReason: Reproduced exactly on the live file. Wireframe collection (VariableCollectionId:2:13) > action/secondary: Wireframe mode = alias:grey/0, Hi-Fi mode = raw #FFFFFF@0% — not an alias. Re-scan of all 34 vars in the collection found the only other non-alias values are the 3 surface/scrim* vars (exempt) and border/danger (separate finding), matching the evidence. Not a scrim, so the intentional exemption does not apply; it breaks the stated both-modes alias rule. Minor is right: zero rendered impact (fully transparent) but a structural breach in the core token system.

figma-tokens.title: Semantic variable border/danger has a raw value in Wireframe mode that also diverges from the status/danger primitive
figma-tokens.detail: border/danger aliases Primitives/status/danger in Hi-Fi mode but holds a raw #C73329 in Wireframe mode — not an alias, and a different red from the status/danger primitive (#B3554D). Not a scrim, so not exempt. Either alias status/danger in both modes or add a wireframe-tier danger primitive.
figma-tokens.verifyReason: Reproduced exactly. border/danger: Wireframe mode = raw #C73329@100% (not an alias), Hi-Fi mode = alias:status/danger; primitive status/danger = #B3554D. Both the alias-rule breach and the color divergence (two different reds, #C73329 vs #B3554D) are confirmed by direct variable dump. Not a scrim, not on the intentional list. Minor is appropriate — it is the one semantic var whose two modes can drift to genuinely different danger reds.

figma-tokens.title: 6 unstyled TEXT nodes on Hi-Fi screens page (excluding the 3 known-intentional NUX wordmarks)
figma-tokens.detail: 9 TEXT nodes have textStyleId === '' (out of 156 text nodes outside instance interiors, shelf excluded). 3 are NUX wordmarks (554:1255, 592:500, 595:917 — known-intentional, not flagged). The remaining 6 are real drift: (1) Game Detail — iPhone > Content > header > meta 'Action-Adventure · 2026 · Cloud Play' (585:1315) is hand-set Inter Medium 12 — a near-miss of HiFi/Metadata (Inter Medium 12.5), so it silently diverges from the style; (2) Film Detail — Desktop > Content > Tabs: 4 tab labels 'More Like This'/'Cast & Crew'/'Details'/'Reviews' (595:832/835/838/841) are unstyled Inter Semi Bold 22 — coincidentally identical to tvOS/Label metrics; no desktop tab style exists in the ramp; (3) Film Detail — Desktop > Content > Details heading (595:863) is unstyled Inter Semi Bold 28 matching no local style (HiFi/Page Title is 34). Apply existing styles or add the missing HiFi tab/section-label styles.
figma-tokens.evidence: unstyledCount=9 of 156 TEXT; fonts: Inter Medium 12 (vs style 12.5), Inter Semi Bold 22 ×4, Inter Semi Bold 28. Plus Jakarta Sans usage on this page: 0.
figma-tokens.verifyReason: Reproduced exactly. Scan of page 571:1404 (shelf x<-1000 and instance interiors excluded): 156 TEXT nodes, 9 with textStyleId === ''. Three are the intentional NUX wordmarks (554:1255, 592:500, 595:917 — correctly not flagged). The remaining 6 match the finding precisely: 585:1315 'Action-Adventure · 2026 · Cloud Play' Inter Medium 12 (local HiFi/Metadata style is Inter Medium 12.5 — confirmed near-miss); 595:832/835/838/841 desktop tab labels Inter Semi Bold 22 (matches tvOS/Label metrics; no desktop tab style exists in the local ramp — verified); 595:863 'Details' Inter Semi Bold 28 (no local Inter 28 style; HiFi/Page Title is 34). PJS usage on the page = 0, as claimed. Minor is right.

figma-tokens.title: Wireframe TabBar variants carry 8 raw DROP_SHADOW effects instead of an effect style
figma-tokens.where: 🧩 Components (1:2) > Showcase > Section/ORGANISMS > TabBar > Selected=Home/Browse/MyList > Frame (95:16, 95:50, 95:55, 95:86, 95:91, 95:122 + 2 more)
figma-tokens.detail: The wireframe TabBar's floating-bar frames use hard-coded DROP_SHADOW effects while every peer component on the page uses shared effect styles (Focus/Ring ×22, Focus/Ring Inverse ×8, Elevation/Card Hover ×9, Focus/tvOS Lift ×5). The Hi-Fi TabBar equivalent is clean (uses HiFi/Glass). Promote the shadow to an effect style or apply an existing one.
figma-tokens.evidence: rawEffects n=8 on page 1:2, all inside TabBar variant frames; rawEffects n=0 on 557:334 and 571:1404.
figma-tokens.verifyReason: Reproduced exactly. Page 1:2 scan (instance interiors excluded): exactly 8 nodes with effects and empty effectStyleId, all DROP_SHADOW, all at path Showcase > Section/ORGANISMS > grid > tile > TabBar > Selected=Home/Browse/MyList/Profile > Frame (95:16, 95:50, 95:55, 95:86, 95:91, 95:122, 95:127, 95:158). Raw-effect count on 557:334 and 571:1404 is 0, confirming the Hi-Fi equivalents are clean. Shared effect styles (Focus/Ring, Focus/Ring Inverse, Elevation/Card Hover, Focus/tvOS Lift, HiFi/Glass) exist and are the page norm. Nit is the right severity for a wireframe-tier styling inconsistency.

figma-tokens.title: Effect-style colors are hard-coded hex, and Focus/Ring's dark stop drifts 1 bit from grey/900
figma-tokens.where: Local effect styles: Focus/Ring (S:c2de5169…), HiFi/Focus Ring (S:cc3a192b…)
figma-tokens.detail: Effect styles duplicate token values as raw hex instead of binding color variables: HiFi/Focus Ring hard-codes #C8922A (amber/500) and #0D0C0B (ink/900) — if amber/500 ever shifts, the focus ring won't follow. Additionally Focus/Ring's dark spread uses #1C1C1F while the grey/900 primitive is #1C1C1E — a 1-bit blue-channel drift indicating it was typed by hand rather than derived from the token.
figma-tokens.evidence: Effect dump: Focus/Ring = [DROP_SHADOW s4 #1C1C1F@100%, DROP_SHADOW s2 #FFFFFF@100%]; grey/900 = #1C1C1E. HiFi/Focus Ring = [s4 #C8922A@100%, s2 #0D0C0B@100%].
figma-tokens.verifyReason: Reproduced exactly. Effect-style dump shows boundVariables = none on every effect of all 8 local effect styles. Focus/Ring (S:c2de5169) = [DROP_SHADOW spread 4 #1C1C1F@100%, spread 2 #FFFFFF@100%]; grey/900 primitive = #1C1C1E — the 1-bit blue-channel drift (0x1F vs 0x1E) is real and consistent with a hand-typed value. HiFi/Focus Ring (S:cc3a192b) = [spread 4 #C8922A@100%, spread 2 #0D0C0B@100%], duplicating amber/500 and ink/900 as raw hex with no variable binding, so an amber/500 change would not propagate. Nit is right — values currently render correctly; this is future-drift risk plus one invisible 1-bit divergence.

figma-tokens.title: Design System page Foundations doc frames use off-scale unbound corner radii (10/20px)
figma-tokens.where: 🎨 Design System (0:1) > 🎨 Foundations board 96:70 (radius 20) and ~30 swatch frames e.g. 96:75/96:79/96:83 (radius 10)
figma-tokens.detail: 31 non-instance frames on the Design System page have non-zero corner radius neither bound to a radius/* variable nor on the radius scale (10 and 20 sit between radius/sm=8, radius/md=12, radius/lg=16). These are documentation-board chrome, not shipped components, but the token spec page itself not eating its own dog food is worth a sweep.
figma-tokens.evidence: radius n=31 on page 0:1; values 20 (board) and 10 (palette swatches). Note: the radius=5 hits initially flagged on 1:2/557:334 were verified to be COMPONENT_SET variant containers (Figma's default purple-frame radius) — false positive, excluded.
figma-tokens.verifyReason: Substance reproduced; count slightly overstated. On page 0:1, 96:70 (🎨 Foundations board) has unbound radius 20 and the palette swatch frames (96:75, 96:79, 96:83, …) have unbound radius 10 — I measure 20 frames at r=10 plus 1 at r=20 (21 nodes at the cited 10/20 values, vs the claimed ~31; my full off-scale total is 28 including 6 at r=3 and 1 at r=28 the finding did not cite). Radius scale confirmed as {4,6,8,12,16,24,32,9999}, so 10 and 20 are genuinely off-scale and 96:70/96:75-class nodes are genuinely unbound. The COMPONENT_SET radius=5 false-positive exclusion also checks out (I excluded them and found none). Documentation chrome only — nit is correct despite the count delta.

figma-tokens.title: 2 unstyled demo texts in the Hi-Fi Foundations amber row on the Design System page
figma-tokens.where: 🎨 Design System (0:1) > 🌒 Hi-Fi Foundations > amber > amber-row > cta 'Play' (556:6) and ghost 'My List' (556:8)

figma-tokens.evidence: Page 0:1 scan: unstyledText n=2 of 103 TEXT nodes; both Inter Semi Bold 15.

Page 0:1 scan: 103 TEXT nodes outside instances, exactly 2 with empty textStyleId — 556:6 'Play' and 556:8 'My List' at 🌒 Hi-Fi Foundations > amber > amber-row > cta/ghost, both raw Inter Semi Bold 15. HiFi/Card Title (Inter Semi Bold 15) exists with identical metrics, so a style could be applied trivially. Tiny spec-board specimens — nit is the right severity.

figma-tokens.title: Wireframe Player iPhone status-bar time '9:41' is the only unstyled text on the wireframes page
figma-tokens.detail: One stray unstyled TEXT node (out of 156) — every other wireframe screen's status bar text is styled or lives inside the StatusBar component instance. Apply the matching style or swap in the StatusBar component for consistency with the other 32 screens.
figma-tokens.evidence: Page 616:334 scan: unstyledText n=1; rawFill/rawStroke/pad/radius all 0; PJS hits (n=36) are the intended wireframe font, not flagged.
figma-tokens.verifyReason: Reproduced exactly. Page 616:334 scan (instance interiors excluded): 156 TEXT nodes, exactly 1 unstyled — 627:2555 '9:41' (Inter Semi Bold 15) under Player — iPhone. The 36 Plus Jakarta Sans hits on this page are the intended wireframe font per the known-intentional list and were correctly not flagged. One stray node out of 33 screens — nit is right.

figma-tokens:refuted: Leftover staging frame '🎯 SF Source — Batch 3 (paste here)' pollutes the 🧩 Components library page — The raw-token hits do reproduce (3 raw SOLID fills #BDBDBD/#1C1C1F/#6E6E73, the page's only 2 unstyled texts 308:158/308:159, 1 unbound padding 40/40/40/40 + itemSpacing 20, all inside 308:157), but the finding's load-bearing claim is factually wrong: the frame is parked at x = -3000, y = 40 — well past the x < -1000 parking line. The finding explicitly asserts 'Unlike the hi-fi asset shelf it is not parked at x < -1000', which measurement contradicts. It therefore matches the file's established off-canvas-parking convention for intentional staging/scratch areas (same pattern as the known-intentional asset shelf), and the suggested fix ('move it off-page') is already effectively in place. The visible working area of the Components page is token-clean. At most this is housekeeping (delete the scratch frame eventually), not a token violation.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Player plays off-catalog title 'The Substance' (which Search says doesn't exist)
figma-visual-iphone.detail: The player's title reads 'The Substance' — not in the canonical catalog. Worse, the NoResults screen explicitly shows the query 'substanse' returning zero results ('We couldn't find anything for "substanse"'), so the app is shown playing a film its own search says it doesn't have. The video surface is also pure black with no film still, which compounds the unfinished look. Swap the player title to a catalog film and ideally add a dimmed frame still.
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: Crop /tmp/nux-audit/z6-player-status.png shows 'The Substance' centered in the player top bar; /tmp/nux-audit/noresults.png shows the 'substanse' no-results state.
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Core fact confirmed: Player 588:1251 top bar is TEXT 'The Substance' (Inter Semi Bold 16, x97 y61) — not in the 21-film catalog, MANIFEST.json, or EXTRAS; it is leftover wireframe filler ('The Substance'/'The Bear' everywhere, per memory) that escaped the hi-fi real-content pass. But both aggravators justifying 'major' fail: (1) NoResults 585:1257 shows a deliberately MISSPELLED query — copy reads 'We couldn't find anything for "substanse". Check the spelling…' — a misspelled-query demo, which does not assert the film is absent (if anything the pairing is coherent); (2) the pure-black video surface matches the established dark fullscreen player treatment on both wireframe and hi-fi players (and tvOS), a scope choice not a defect. What remains is one off-universe text string fixable with a single override → minor.

figma-visual-iphone.title: CourseDetail rating value missing/clipped after star in metadata row
figma-visual-iphone.where: iPhone CourseDetail 585:1341 (meta row under 'The Art of Editing')
figma-visual-iphone.detail: Meta row reads 'COURSE | by A. Okonkwo · 12 lessons · 4h 30m (18) ★' — the star glyph is the last element, sitting ~15px from the right screen edge with NO numeric rating after it. Sibling detail screens show '★ 7.1' (FilmDetail 581:1283, GameDetail 585:1302). The row is too long for 393pt and the rating number is clipped or its text node is empty. Either shorten the row (e.g. drop the '18' pill — an age rating on an editing course is also questionable content) or wrap/move the rating.
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: Pixel-level crop /tmp/nux-audit/z8-course-star-edge.png shows star then solid black to the frame edge; PIL scan found 0 light pixels right of x=378. Compare /tmp/nux-audit/z7-fd-titlemeta.png ('★ 7.1' fits with margin on FilmDetail).
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Reproduced and measured. The rating text is NOT empty — TEXT '7.1' (Inter Regular 14) exists at x=383 w=16 (extends to x=399 on a 393pt screen) inside HiFi/Rating instance (x=365 w=34), but the parent 'meta' frame clips at x=377 (x16 + w361), so '7.1' is 100% clipped and even the star (ic/star.fill x365–379) loses its last 2px. Rendered-pixel scan of the live render: 0 light pixels at x≥378 in the meta band; rightmost light pixel x=376. Visible truncated content on a hi-fi detail screen where sibling FilmDetail/GameDetail show '★ 7.1' intact — major stands.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Home hero has no top scrim — status bar, NUX wordmark and header icons barely legible on light poster art
figma-visual-iphone.detail: White status glyphs (wifi/battery especially), the white serif NUX wordmark, and the borderless white cast + avatar header icons all sit directly on the light tan 'LAWRENCE OF ARABIA' poster lettering/sky with no darkening scrim. Contrast is far below comfortable legibility. FilmDetail 581:1283 handles the same problem correctly with a dark top scrim behind its status bar. Add the same top gradient scrim to the Home hero.
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: Crop /tmp/nux-audit/z10-home-top.png — battery/wifi icons over cream 'OF ARABIA' area, white-on-tan; compare /tmp/nux-audit/z6-fd-status.png where FilmDetail status glyphs sit on a dimmed band.
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Legibility failure reproduced and measured, though the mechanism claim is wrong: Home hero 554:1251 carries the IDENTICAL top scrim as FilmDetail 581:1283 (GRADIENT_LINEAR rgba(13,12,11) alpha 0.6→0 over the top 20%, xform [[0,1,0],[-1,0,1]]) — FD only reads better because its art is darker. The scrim decays to ~0 by y≈94 while the wordmark/header row sits at y60–91 over light poster sky: measured background luminance behind status icons med 0.42–0.59 → white glyphs ≈1.65–2.2:1; behind the NUX wordmark med 0.79 → ≈1.25:1; behind cast/avatar icons up to 0.88 → ≈1.12:1 (near-invisible, confirmed visually). Real major defect; fix = strengthen/extend the existing scrim, not add a missing one.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Player status bar shows time but drops the right icon cluster
figma-visual-iphone.detail: Only '9:41' renders at top-left; signal/wifi/battery are absent (every other screen shows the full set), and the home indicator is also missing. If the intent is immersive playback chrome, the time should hide too; if not, the right cluster should be present. Current half-hidden state reads as an unfinished status bar component.
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: Crop /tmp/nux-audit/z6-player-status.png: '9:41' present, top-right corner empty above the AirPlay icon.
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Reproduced programmatically: Player 588:1251 top-level children are [Icon(close), 'The Substance', Icon(airplay), transport, Icon×3, HiFi/Scrubber, '9:41'] — a bare '9:41' TEXT at x24 y14 with no StatusBar instance; findAll for wifi/battery/cellular/HomeIndicator returns 0 nodes, and the rendered top-right corner is empty. Every other hi-fi iPhone screen carries the full HiFi/StatusBar + HiFi/HomeIndicator. The half-state (time without cluster, no home indicator) reads unfinished rather than deliberately immersive — minor confirmed.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Offline screen CTA says 'Browse' while copy points to Downloads
figma-visual-iphone.detail: Supporting copy reads 'Check your connection and try again. Downloaded titles are still available.' but the single amber CTA is 'Browse' — an action that requires a network connection and which the copy doesn't mention. The CTA should be 'Go to Downloads' (or 'Retry'), matching the promise in the copy. The identical 'Browse' button is correct on NoResults/MyList but was seemingly copy-pasted here.
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: /tmp/nux-audit/offline.png — wifi-slash icon, 'You're offline', Downloads-referencing copy, amber 'Browse' button.
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Reproduced: Offline 585:1297 texts = 'You're offline' / 'Check your connection and try again. Downloaded titles are still available.' / single CTA label 'Browse' (y508). Browse needs a connection; the copy promises Downloads and a retry, neither of which the CTA offers. Note the wireframe Offline (228:534) used 'Retry', supporting the copy-paste hypothesis from the NoResults/MyList empty states. Minor is right.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Downloads rows use inconsistent metadata patterns
figma-visual-iphone.detail: Lawrence of Arabia shows genre + size ('Epic · 2.1 GB'), Aftersun shows size only ('1.1 GB'), The Wicker Man shows size + expiry ('1.4 GB · Expires in 3 days'), Senna shows state only ('Downloading · 62%'). Genre appears on exactly one row. Pick one schema (e.g. size always; genre always or never; expiry/state as a second element) and apply it to all four rows. Poster thumbnails themselves are honest (LoA art, Senna yellow poster, Aftersun still, burning Wicker Man).
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: /tmp/nux-audit/downloads.png — compare subtitle lines of the four rows.
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Reproduced from text nodes on 588:1396: 'Epic · 2.1 GB' (Lawrence) / 'Downloading · 62%' (Senna) / '1.1 GB' (Aftersun) / '1.4 GB · Expires in 3 days' (Wicker Man). The state variety (downloading, expiring) is defensible showcase content, but the two SAME-state completed rows use different schemas — genre appears on exactly one of four rows — which is genuine metadata-schema drift within one list. Minor confirmed.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Profile and Settings use different list-row treatments
figma-visual-iphone.detail: Profile menu rows (Downloads, Notifications, Subscription & Billing, Settings, Help & Support, Sign out) are flat full-bleed rows with hairline separators and no container, while Settings rows sit in rounded grouped cards with PLAYBACK/NOTIFICATIONS/ACCOUNT section headers. Two adjacent utility screens in the same flow should share one list component (the Settings grouped-card pattern looks like the more finished one).
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: Crops /tmp/nux-audit/z9-profile-rows.png (flat hairline rows) vs /tmp/nux-audit/z9-settings-rows.png (rounded card groups).
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Reproduced visually: Profile 588:1310 = flat full-bleed rows with hairline separators; Settings 588:1351 = rounded inset-grouped cards under PLAYBACK/NOTIFICATIONS/ACCOUNT headers. However both are canonical iOS list idioms — plain lists for navigation menus, inset-grouped for settings controls — and Apple's own apps differentiate exactly this way (HIG-conformant), so the split is defensible by screen purpose rather than an unfinished state. A shared row component would be tidier; that makes it a nit, not minor.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Auth screen headings set in Fraunces while every other functional screen uses Inter
figma-visual-iphone.where: iPhone SignIn 588:1268 ('Welcome back') and SignUp 588:1288 ('Create your account')
figma-visual-iphone.detail: Per the typography rule, Fraunces is for content/theme headings and Inter for functional UI. Browse, My List, Profile, Settings, Downloads all use Inter bold large titles, but both auth headings are serif (Fraunces). If this is a deliberate 'brand moment' it should be documented; otherwise switch to Inter to match the functional-screen heading style. The two auth screens are at least consistent with each other (fields, Apple button, link rows all align).
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: /tmp/nux-audit/signin.png and /tmp/nux-audit/signup.png serif headings vs sans titles in /tmp/nux-audit/browse.png, /tmp/nux-audit/settings.png.
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Reproduced: 'Welcome back' and 'Create your account' are Fraunces SemiBold 28, vs Inter Semi Bold 34 large titles on Browse/My List/Profile/Settings/Downloads. But the finding missed that BOTH desktop onboarding screens also head in Fraunces (NUX 36; 'Cinema for Curious Minds' 40; 'What kinds of stories move you?' 40) — auth + onboarding form a consistent brand-surface set, the same four roots that deliberately received the brand grain overlay. The serif treatment is therefore a coherent brand-moment convention across all welcome surfaces, not one-off drift; at most it deserves a one-line documentation note → nit.

figma-visual-iphone.title: Active 'All' filter chip shows a remove (X) affordance
figma-visual-iphone.where: iPhone Browse 581:1251 and NoResults 585:1257 (filter chip row)
figma-visual-iphone.detail: The selected 'All' chip renders with an X-to-remove icon, but 'All' is the default/unfiltered state — removing it is semantically meaningless. The X affordance belongs on actual applied filters (Films, a Genre, etc.), not on 'All'. Both screens are at least consistent with each other.
figma-visual-iphone.evidence: /tmp/nux-audit/browse.png and /tmp/nux-audit/noresults.png — amber 'All ✕' chip at left of the chip row.
figma-visual-iphone.verifyReason: Reproduced programmatically and visually: on Browse 581:1251 the 'All' chip is FilterChip {Type: Selected, State: Default} with a visible ic/xmark (amber 'All ✕'), and NoResults 585:1257 shows the same selected 'All'. The X is baked into the design-system Selected variant, but on the default unfiltered 'All' state a dismiss affordance is semantically meaningless — the component would need an X-less Selected variant for defaults. Correctly scoped as a nit.

figma-visual-iphone:refuted: Off-catalog film 'Get Carter' in Continue Watching rail — Reproduced visually (2nd CW card = Michael Caine still labeled 'Get Carter / 47m left') but the premise is wrong. The project's documented content rule is still↔own-film honesty + genre-section matching, not membership in the web app's 21-title FILMS array. Project memory (2026-06-12 category-mismatch fix) explicitly rules: 'In Figma Continue rows Get Carter / Matter of Life and Death are fine — labels match their stills (those films exist in the mock catalog).' Get Carter has two entries in design/posters/MANIFEST.json (the 35-entry Figma content pool). The pair is honest and owner-accepted — intentional, not a defect.

Search for 'the third man' surfaces The Red Shoes as a Top Result, with a monochrome thumbnail — Content reproduces ('the third man' → The Third Man, Film · 1949 + The Red Shoes, Film · 1948) but it is the documented content map from the real-content pass ('Search query «the third man» → Third Man/Red Shoes results'), and the b/w Red Shoes rehearsal still was deliberately sourced in the landscape-stills batch for search slots. A static mock does not model a relevance algorithm — a second loosely-related row is standard demo filler — and the finding itself concedes the thumb is honest. No defect to act on.

Hero 'My List' action sits bare over busy poster faces — Premise wrong: the CTA is not unscrimmed. The hero's 5-stop bottom scrim Rectangle (y140–472) sits directly behind the CTA row ('My List' label at y401), and measured legibility is comfortable — background luminance behind/around the label: median 0.12, p90 0.21 → white label ≈4.0:1 worst-case and ~6:1 typical (the poster's portrait strip is dark there). Scrim layers are explicitly known-intentional layout glue, and a bare icon+label secondary action beside a filled Play pill over scrimmed hero art is the established pattern across FD/Game/Course action rows and standard streaming heroes. 'Fragile against lighter artwork' is hypothetical for a static screen whose art and scrim are fixed.

figma-visual-big.title: Non-catalog films used on Desktop Home and tvOS screens (Get Carter, A Matter of Life and Death)

figma-visual-big.evidence: Desktop CW card labels read 'Get Carter' and 'A Matter of Life and Death' at 2x zoom; identical stills identified on all three tvOS screens.
figma-visual-big.verifyReason: Reproduced via text labels AND image-fill hashes. Hash proof of still reuse: Get Carter fill beec13038b8f appears on Desktop Home CW, tvOS Home CW (x=428), tvOS Detail More Like This (x=1092), and tvOS Browse row 2 (x=760 — the yellow MICHAEL CAINE frame visible in render); AMOLAD fill 77a243b7ceec appears on Desktop Home CW, tvOS Home CW (x=1424), and tvOS Browse row 1 (x=760). The hedged coronation-still claim is correctly marked unprovable (unlabeled hash d566bfe03b39). Severity major is right for non-catalog content on 4 screens.

figma-visual-big.title: tvOS Player plays 'The Substance' — not in catalog and contradicts the No-Results screens
figma-visual-big.detail: Player overlay title is 'The Substance / Thriller · 2026', which is not one of the 21 canonical titles. Worse, the iPad and Desktop Browse No-Results screens (592:623, 595:887) are built around the search 'substanse' returning nothing — so the demo universe simultaneously claims the title doesn't exist and is being played on tvOS.
